title: Build the dedicated Follow-ups view with quick actions and inline cadence control
userStory: As the user, I want a dedicated Follow-ups view listing every posting due for a nudge, most-overdue first, with quick actions, so I can work through who needs following up in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: A new Follow-ups view/tab sits alongside the existing companies | jobs tabs and loads from GET /api/follow-ups.
Each row shows company + title, last outreach date, days overdue, and contacts rendered as mailto links, sorted most-overdue-first.
Quick actions per row: mark replied, mark no-response (both PUT outreach_status and remove the row from the list), and open the pursuit panel (to draft a follow-up via the existing outreach flow).
An inline 'follow up after N days' control in the view header reads/writes the cadence setting; changing it re-fetches and re-renders the list.
Empty state: when nothing is due, the view shows a clear 'no follow-ups due' message rather than a blank/broken list.
Drafting and marking-sent a follow-up from the pursuit panel bumps last_outreach_at (existing behavior), which removes the posting from the due list until the interval elapses again.

[UI changes] Verify in the browser: a posting older than the interval with status=awaiting appears; marking replied removes it; changing the interval changes the list; empty state renders. (Use the tsx build workaround per project memory.)
TypeScript compiles via the project's working build path; no console errors on the Follow-ups view.
